execution_order_note: This case was run SECOND in the procedure v1.0 execution sequence of this session, after case 02. Case 02 was a prior procedure-v1.0 case at execution time. Case 01 had not yet been re-run under procedure v1.0; it was available only in pre-v1.0 form (Round 7) which was not used as a structural reference.

time_to_arbitration.measurement_note: LLM-resident execution; literal minute-bounds not measured. No time-box overruns.
time_to_arbitration.prior_similar_shape_cases_note: Case 02 shares bundle-binary input-frame shape with case 03; content axes differ (reversibility profile in case 02 vs data-coupling depth + infrastructure persistence in case 03).
time_to_arbitration.time_delta_note: Cannot be computed without instrumented timing. Honest data gap about E1.5 instrumentation.

prior_structures_referenced.reuse_note: Reused but with different mechanism — institutional/political-economic irreversibility in case 03 versus ecological/molecular irreversibility in case 02. Same axis-label, different mechanism.

compositional_reuse.fresh_structures_note: Three structurally-fresh contributions: (a) data-coupling-depth as a SECOND decomposition axis combined with reversibility (Step 2 axis 1 and 2 both load-bearing), (b) Step 7 trajectory T4 questioning healthcare-ministry scope-of-authority for civilizational-scale effects (deeper-frame-rejection shape not present in case 02), (c) cross-scale relation: 'individual-scale consent does not aggregate to civilizational-scale consent' (Step 6).

patterns_recognized.refutation_conditions: Per case 02 entry. Two-case occurrence at end of case 03 execution.
Two-case occurrence with different mechanisms (ecological in case 02, institutional in case 03). Suggests axis is domain-elastic, not procedure-imposed identical-mechanism.
Two-case occurrence. Refutation: case where no established alternative exists and procedure still produces this mandate.
Two-case occurrence. Refutation per case 02 entry.
Authored in this case; not yet cross-case validated. Refutation: a case where scope-of-authority is appropriately matched to effect-scale and procedure does not produce this trajectory.

memory_logger_capacity_change_records.description: Frame validation returned `needs decomposition` on data-coupling-depth and infrastructure-persistence grounds.
Two-case occurrence registered for refuse-bundle and reversibility-axis patterns.
Deeper-frame-rejection (questioning scope-of-authority) emerges as candidate trajectory shape. One-case occurrence; preserves as candidate pending cross-case test.
Cross-scale relation: individual-scale consent does not aggregate to civilizational-scale consent. Novel relation for population-scale-infrastructure cases.
memory_logger_capacity_change_records.refutable_with: A case in which population-scale consent IS demonstrably the aggregation of individual-scale consent acts (e.g., explicit democratic vote on the infrastructure).

judgment_acts.reason: Bundle conflates components with categorically different data-coupling depth, consent profile, and infrastructure-persistence. Treating as one decision hides surveillance-infrastructure construction behind opt-in chatbot deployment. Appropriate to this case under these conditions.

Case 03's content requires BOTH axes: data-coupling depth determines what KIND of infrastructure is being built, infrastructure persistence determines how reversible the build is. Single-axis decomposition would hide one or the other. Appropriate to this case under these conditions.
judgment_acts.consequence: Step 2 produced two distinct decomposition families (A/B/C by data-coupling and D/E/F by reversibility); subsequent steps drew on both axes.

article_i_check.note: All patterns registered with confidence ≤0.6 (two-case engagement still preliminary). No truth claim.

article_ii_check.note: Frame Validator returned `needs decomposition` on case 03's specific content axes (data-coupling depth + infrastructure persistence), not on case 02's axis. Reuse of prior structures (Step 9 pattern, etc.) was applied to a freshly-decomposed framing.
